key_insight: MPU-PINNs speed up PINN training by starting on coarse collocation grids and progressively refining to denser grids, while transferring learned parameters between levels.
engineering_relevance: This makes physics-informed solvers more practical for large PDE problems by reducing training cost without sacrificing accuracy.
method: Train one neural network on a coarse set of collocation points first.
Transfer weights to finer collocation levels and continue optimization.
Use a scaling technique to help with spectral bias on high-frequency problems.
evidence_signal: The method was evaluated on 2D/3D Poisson, convection-diffusion-reaction, and Helmholtz benchmarks, where it reportedly cut training time while matching the accuracy of standard PINNs, SA-PINNs, and XPINNs.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the coarse-to-fine schedule and scaling are tuned for each PDE, especially for stiff or highly oscillatory cases.
paper_title: A new strategy for physics-informed neural networks based on hierarchical collocation point refinement
